title.raw: 《循吏列傳》 Biographies of Upright Officials

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：法令所以導民也，刑罰所以禁姦也。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian said: Laws and commands guide the people, punishments prohibit wickedness.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: Laws and decrees exist to guide the people; punishments and penalties exist to prohibit wrongdoing.

sentences.zh: 文武不備，良民懼然身修者，官未曾亂也。

translations.literal: If culture and martial force are not complete, good people will fear and cultivate themselves, officials have never been disorderly.
translations.idiomatic: Even when civil and military institutions are imperfect, virtuous citizens still cultivate themselves out of caution—so long as their officials have never fallen into disorder.

sentences.zh: 奉職循理，亦可以為治，何必威嚴哉？

translations.literal: Devoted to duty and following principle, one can also govern, why must it be severe?
translations.idiomatic: If one faithfully performs one's duties and follows sound principles, good governance will naturally follow. Why must one resort to severity and intimidation?

translations.literal: The Grand Historian said: Laws and commands guide the people, punishments prohibit wickedness. If culture and martial force are not complete, good people will fear and cultivate themselves, officials have never been disorderly. Devoted to duty and following principle, one can also govern, why must it be severe?

sentences.zh: 孫叔敖者，楚之處士也。

translations.literal: Sun Shu Ao was a Chu recluse.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Shuao was a reclusive scholar from the state of Chu.

sentences.zh: 虞丘相進之於楚莊王，以自代也。

translations.literal: Yu Qiu Xiang recommended him to Chu Zhuang Wang to replace himself.
translations.idiomatic: The minister Yu Qiu recommended him to King Zhuang of Chu as his own replacement.

sentences.zh: 三月為楚相，施教導民，上下和合，世俗盛美，政緩禁止，吏無姦邪，盜賊不起。

translations.literal: Three months as Chu prime minister, he implemented teachings to guide the people, superiors and inferiors harmonized, customs flourished beautifully, government was relaxed yet prohibitions held, officials had no wickedness, thieves did not arise.
translations.idiomatic: Within three months of becoming Chancellor of Chu, he implemented policies to educate and guide the people. Superiors and subordinates worked in harmony, public morals flourished, governance was lenient yet prohibitions were respected, officials committed no transgressions, and bandits ceased to arise.

sentences.zh: 秋冬則勸民山採，春夏以水，各得其所便，民皆樂其生。

translations.literal: In autumn and winter he urged people to gather in mountains, in spring and summer by water, each getting what was convenient, people all delighted in their livelihood.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn and winter he encouraged the people to gather resources in the mountains; in spring and summer, to fish and work along the waterways. Everyone found what suited them, and the people all took joy in their livelihoods.

translations.literal: Sun Shu Ao was a Chu recluse. Yu Qiu Xiang recommended him to Chu Zhuang Wang to replace himself. Three months as Chu prime minister, he implemented teachings to guide the people, superiors and inferiors harmonized, customs flourished beautifully, government was relaxed yet prohibitions held, officials had no wickedness, thieves did not arise. In autumn and winter he urged people to gather in mountains, in spring and summer by water, each getting what was convenient, people all delighted in their livelihood.

sentences.zh: 莊王以為幣輕，更以小為大，百姓不便，皆去其業。

translations.literal: Zhuang Wang thought the currency was light, changed small to large, people were inconvenienced, all abandoned their occupations.
translations.idiomatic: King Zhuang decided the currency was too light and replaced the small coins with larger ones. The common people found this inconvenient and abandoned their trades.

sentences.zh: 市令言之相曰：「市亂，民莫安其處，次行不定。」

translations.literal: The market official spoke to the prime minister: 'Market is chaotic, people cannot settle in their places, traders cannot establish their businesses.'
translations.idiomatic: The market superintendent reported to the Chancellor: 'The marketplace is in chaos. The people cannot settle in their stalls, and the rows of merchants are in disarray.'

sentences.zh: 相曰：「如此幾何頃乎？」

translations.literal: Prime minister said: 'How long has this been?'
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor asked, 'How long has this been going on?'

sentences.zh: 市令曰：「三月頃。」

translations.literal: Market official said: 'About three months.'
translations.idiomatic: The market superintendent replied, 'About three months.'

sentences.zh: 相曰：「罷，吾今令之復矣。」

translations.literal: Prime minister said: 'Stop, I will now order it restored.'
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor said, 'Enough. I shall now have the old currency restored.'

sentences.zh: 後五日，朝，相言之王曰：「前日更幣，以為輕。

translations.literal: After five days, at court, prime minister spoke to the king: 'Previously changing currency because it was light.
translations.idiomatic: Five days later, at the morning audience, the Chancellor addressed the king: 'Recently the currency was changed because it was deemed too light.

sentences.zh: 今市令來言曰『市亂，民莫安其處，次行之不定』。

translations.literal: Now market official comes saying 'market chaotic, people cannot settle in places, traders cannot establish businesses'.
translations.idiomatic: Now the market superintendent has come to report that the marketplace is in chaos, the people cannot settle in their stalls, and the merchants' rows are in disarray.

sentences.zh: 臣請遂令復如故。」

translations.literal: I request to then order restoration as before.'
translations.idiomatic: I respectfully request that we issue an order to restore the former system.'

sentences.zh: 王許之，下令三日而市復如故。

translations.literal: King allowed it, issued order three days and market restored as before.
translations.idiomatic: The king approved his request. Within three days of the order being issued, the marketplace returned to normal.

translations.literal: Zhuang Wang thought the currency was light, changed small to large, people were inconvenienced, all abandoned their occupations. The market official spoke to the prime minister: 'Market is chaotic, people cannot settle in their places, traders cannot establish their businesses.' Prime minister said: 'How long has this been?' Market official said: 'About three months.' Prime minister said: 'Stop, I will now order it restored.' After five days, at court, prime minister spoke to the king: 'Previously changing currency because it was light. Now market official comes saying 'market chaotic, people cannot settle in places, traders cannot establish businesses'. I request to then order restoration as before.' King allowed it, issued order three days and market restored as before.

sentences.zh: 楚民俗好庳車，王以為庳車不便馬，欲下令使高之。

translations.literal: Chu people custom liked low chariots, king thought low chariots inconvenienced horses, wished to issue order to raise them.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Chu customarily favored low-slung carriages. The king considered these inconvenient for the horses and wished to issue a decree requiring them to be raised.

sentences.zh: 相曰：「令數下，民不知所從，不可。

translations.literal: Prime minister said: 'Orders issued frequently, people do not know what to follow, cannot.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor said, 'If decrees are issued too frequently, the people will not know which to follow. This will not do.

sentences.zh: 王必欲高車，臣請教閭里使高其梱。

translations.literal: If king must raise chariots, I request to teach neighborhoods to raise their axles.
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty insists on raising the carriages, allow me to instruct the neighborhoods to raise their door thresholds instead.

sentences.zh: 乘車者皆君子，君子不能數下車。」

translations.literal: Riders are all gentlemen, gentlemen cannot frequently alight from chariots.'
translations.idiomatic: Those who ride in carriages are all gentlemen, and gentlemen cannot be constantly stepping down from their carriages.'

sentences.zh: 王許之。

translations.literal: King allowed it.
translations.idiomatic: The king approved.

sentences.zh: 居半歲，民悉自高其車。

translations.literal: After half a year, people all raised their own chariots.
translations.idiomatic: Within half a year, the people had all raised their carriages of their own accord.

translations.literal: Chu people custom liked low chariots, king thought low chariots inconvenienced horses, wished to issue order to raise them. Prime minister said: 'Orders issued frequently, people do not know what to follow, cannot. If king must raise chariots, I request to teach neighborhoods to raise their axles. Riders are all gentlemen, gentlemen cannot frequently alight from chariots.' King allowed it. After half a year, people all raised their own chariots.

sentences.zh: 此不教而民從其化，近者視而效之，遠者四面望而法之。

translations.literal: This did not teach yet people followed their transformation, near ones saw and imitated, far ones from four sides gazed and modeled after.
translations.idiomatic: In this way, without issuing edicts, the people followed his transformative influence. Those nearby observed and imitated him; those far away looked on from all directions and modeled themselves after him.

sentences.zh: 故三得相而不喜，知其材自得之也；

translations.literal: Therefore three times obtained prime minister yet not happy, knew his talent self-obtained;
translations.idiomatic: Thus, when he was appointed Chancellor three times, he felt no elation, knowing that his ability had earned it naturally;

sentences.zh: 三去相而不悔，知非己之罪也。

translations.literal: Three times left prime minister yet not regretted, knew not his own crime.
translations.idiomatic: and when he was dismissed three times, he felt no regret, knowing it was not due to any fault of his own.

translations.literal: This did not teach yet people followed their transformation, near ones saw and imitated, far ones from four sides gazed and modeled after. Therefore three times obtained prime minister yet not happy, knew his talent self-obtained; Three times left prime minister yet not regretted, knew not his own crime.

sentences.zh: 子產者，鄭之列大夫也。

translations.literal: Zi Chan was Zheng's ranking grand master.
translations.idiomatic: Zi Chan was a senior grandee of the state of Zheng.

sentences.zh: 鄭昭君之時，以所愛徐摯為相，國亂，上下不親，父子不和。

translations.literal: During Zheng Zhao Jun's time, because he favored Xu Zhi as prime minister, state was chaotic, superiors and inferiors not close, fathers and sons not harmonious.
translations.idiomatic: In the time of Duke Zhao of Zheng, the duke appointed his favorite Xu Zhi as chief minister. The state fell into disorder: superiors and subordinates were estranged, and fathers and sons were at odds.

sentences.zh: 大宮子期言之君，以子產為相。

translations.literal: Da Gong Zi Qi spoke to the jun, made Zi Chan prime minister.
translations.idiomatic: Dagong Ziqi spoke to the duke and had Zi Chan appointed as chief minister.

sentences.zh: 為相一年，豎子不戲狎，斑白不提挈，僮子不犁畔。

translations.literal: As prime minister one year, youths did not play improperly, elderly did not carry burdens, children did not plow boundaries.
translations.idiomatic: After one year as minister, young men no longer engaged in reckless mischief, the gray-haired elderly no longer had to carry heavy loads, and children no longer labored at plowing the fields.

sentences.zh: 二年，市不豫賈。

translations.literal: Two years, market not falsely priced.
translations.idiomatic: After two years, merchants in the marketplace no longer cheated on prices.

sentences.zh: 三年，門不夜關，道不拾遺。

translations.literal: Three years, gates not closed at night, roads not picked up lost items.
translations.idiomatic: After three years, gates were no longer bolted at night, and no one picked up lost items from the road.

sentences.zh: 四年，田器不歸。

translations.literal: Four years, farming tools not returned.
translations.idiomatic: After four years, farmers left their tools in the fields without bringing them home.

sentences.zh: 五年，士無尺籍，喪期不令而治。

translations.literal: Five years, scholars no false records, mourning periods not commanded yet orderly.
translations.idiomatic: After five years, soldiers had no need for conscription rolls, and mourning rites were observed in proper order without any commands being issued.

sentences.zh: 治鄭二十六年而死，丁壯號哭，老人兒啼，曰：「子產去我死乎！

translations.literal: Governed Zheng twenty six years and died, able-bodied men wailed, elders wept like children, said: 'Zi Chan leaves us to die!
translations.idiomatic: He governed Zheng for twenty-six years before he died. The young and strong wailed aloud, and the old wept like children, crying: 'Zi Chan has left us—we are lost!

sentences.zh: 民將安歸？」

translations.literal: People will go where?'
translations.idiomatic: Where shall the people turn now?'

translations.literal: Zi Chan was Zheng's ranking grand master. During Zheng Zhao Jun's time, because he favored Xu Zhi as prime minister, state was chaotic, superiors and inferiors not close, fathers and sons not harmonious. Da Gong Zi Qi spoke to the jun, made Zi Chan prime minister. As prime minister one year, youths did not play improperly, elderly did not carry burdens, children did not plow boundaries. Two years, market not falsely priced. Three years, gates not closed at night, roads not picked up lost items. Four years, farming tools not returned. Five years, scholars no false records, mourning periods not commanded yet orderly. Governed Zheng twenty six years and died, able-bodied men wailed, elders wept like children, said: 'Zi Chan leaves us to die! People will go where?'

sentences.zh: 公儀休者，魯博士也。

translations.literal: Gong Yi Xiu was a Lu doctor.
translations.idiomatic: Gongyi Xiu was a scholar-official of the state of Lu.

sentences.zh: 以高弟為魯相。

translations.literal: With high merit became Lu prime minister.
translations.idiomatic: By virtue of his outstanding scholarship, he was appointed Chancellor of Lu.

sentences.zh: 奉法循理，無所變更，百官自正。

translations.literal: Devoted to law following principle, no changes, hundred officials self-corrected.
translations.idiomatic: He upheld the law and followed sound principles, making no arbitrary changes, and all the officials naturally corrected their own conduct.

sentences.zh: 使食祿者不得與下民爭利，受大者不得取小。

translations.literal: Made salary receivers not compete with common people for profit, receiving great not take small.
translations.idiomatic: He ensured that those who received government salaries did not compete with the common people for profit, and that those in high positions did not seize petty gains.

translations.literal: Gong Yi Xiu was a Lu doctor. With high merit became Lu prime minister. Devoted to law following principle, no changes, hundred officials self-corrected. Made salary receivers not compete with common people for profit, receiving great not take small.

sentences.zh: 客有遺相魚者，相不受。

translations.literal: Guest had one who gifted prime minister fish, prime minister not receive.
translations.idiomatic: A visitor once presented the Chancellor with a gift of fish, but the Chancellor refused to accept it.

sentences.zh: 客曰：「聞君嗜魚，遺君魚，何故不受也？」

translations.literal: Guest said: 'Heard jun likes fish, gifted jun fish, why not receive?'
translations.idiomatic: The visitor said, 'I have heard that you are fond of fish, so I brought you some. Why do you refuse?'

sentences.zh: 相曰：「以嗜魚，故不受也。

translations.literal: Prime minister said: 'Because like fish, therefore not receive.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor replied, 'It is precisely because I am fond of fish that I refuse.

sentences.zh: 今為相，能自給魚；

translations.literal: Now as prime minister, can self-supply fish;
translations.idiomatic: As Chancellor, I can provide my own fish;

sentences.zh: 今受魚而免，誰復給我魚者？

translations.literal: Now receive fish and resign, who again supply me fish?
translations.idiomatic: but if I accept your fish and am dismissed from office, who then would supply me with fish?

sentences.zh: 吾故不受也。」

translations.literal: I therefore not receive.'
translations.idiomatic: That is why I refuse.'

translations.literal: Guest had one who gifted prime minister fish, prime minister not receive. Guest said: 'Heard jun likes fish, gifted jun fish, why not receive?' Prime minister said: 'Because like fish, therefore not receive. Now as prime minister, can self-supply fish; Now receive fish and resign, who again supply me fish? I therefore not receive.'

sentences.zh: 食茹而美，拔其園葵而棄之。

translations.literal: Ate greens yet beautiful, pulled his garden mallows and abandoned them.
translations.idiomatic: When he tasted the vegetables from his garden and found them delicious, he uprooted the mallows growing there and threw them away.

sentences.zh: 見其家織布好，而疾出其家婦，燔其機，云「欲令農士工女安所讎其貨乎」？

translations.literal: Saw his family weave cloth well, and angrily expelled his family woman, burned her loom, said 'want to make farmers scholars artisans women where sell their goods?'
translations.idiomatic: When he saw that his household produced fine cloth, he angrily sent away his wife, burned her loom, and declared, 'How would you have the farmers and craftswomen find a market for their goods?'

translations.literal: Ate greens yet beautiful, pulled his garden mallows and abandoned them. Saw his family weave cloth well, and angrily expelled his family woman, burned her loom, said 'want to make farmers scholars artisans women where sell their goods?'

sentences.zh: 石奢者，楚昭王相也。

translations.literal: Shi She was Chu Zhao Wang's prime minister.
translations.idiomatic: Shi She was the Chancellor of King Zhao of Chu.

sentences.zh: 堅直廉正，無所阿避。

translations.literal: Firm upright incorruptible, no partiality avoidance.
translations.idiomatic: He was firm, upright, incorruptible, and just, showing neither favoritism nor evasion.

sentences.zh: 行縣，道有殺人者，相追之，乃其父也。

translations.literal: Patrolling commandery, road had murderer, prime minister pursued, was his father.
translations.idiomatic: While on an inspection tour of the counties, he encountered a murderer on the road. The Chancellor gave chase, only to discover it was his own father.

sentences.zh: 縱其父而還自系焉。

translations.literal: Released his father and returned self-bound.
translations.idiomatic: He released his father, then returned and placed himself in bonds.

sentences.zh: 使人言之王曰：「殺人者，臣之父也。

translations.literal: Made person speak to wang: 'Murderer, minister's father.
translations.idiomatic: He sent a messenger to inform the king: 'The murderer is your servant's father.

sentences.zh: 夫以父立政，不孝也；

translations.literal: With father establish politics, not filial;
translations.idiomatic: To enforce the law upon one's own father would be unfilial;

sentences.zh: 廢法縱罪，非忠也；

translations.literal: Waste law release crime, not loyal;
translations.idiomatic: to set aside the law and release a criminal would be disloyal;

sentences.zh: 臣罪當死。」

translations.literal: Minister crime deserves death.'
translations.idiomatic: your servant's crime deserves death.'

sentences.zh: 王曰：「追而不及，不當伏罪，子其治事矣。」

translations.literal: Wang said: 'Pursued yet not caught, should not bear crime, son manage affairs.'
translations.idiomatic: The king said, 'You gave chase but did not catch him. You should bear no guilt. Return to your duties.'

sentences.zh: 石奢曰：「不私其父，非孝子也；

translations.literal: Shi She said: 'Not favor own father, not filial son;
translations.idiomatic: Shi She replied, 'A man who shows no partiality toward his own father is no filial son;

sentences.zh: 不奉主法，非忠臣也。

translations.literal: Not follow jun law, not loyal minister.
translations.idiomatic: a man who does not uphold his lord's law is no loyal minister.

sentences.zh: 王赦其罪，上惠也；

translations.literal: Wang pardon his crime, upper benevolence;
translations.idiomatic: That Your Majesty pardons my crime is a sovereign's grace;

sentences.zh: 伏誅而死，臣職也。」

translations.literal: Bear execution die, minister duty.'
translations.idiomatic: but to submit to execution and die is a minister's duty.'

sentences.zh: 遂不受令，自刎而死。

translations.literal: Then not accept order, self-cut throat die.
translations.idiomatic: He refused the king's pardon and cut his own throat.

translations.literal: Shi She was Chu Zhao Wang's prime minister. Firm upright incorruptible, no partiality avoidance. Patrolling commandery, road had murderer, prime minister pursued, was his father. Released his father and returned self-bound. Made person speak to wang: 'Murderer, minister's father. With father establish politics, not filial; Waste law release crime, not loyal; Minister crime deserves death.' Wang said: 'Pursued yet not caught, should not bear crime, son manage affairs.' Shi She said: 'Not favor own father, not filial son; Not follow jun law, not loyal minister. Wang pardon his crime, upper benevolence; Bear execution die, minister duty.' Then not accept order, self-cut throat die.

sentences.zh: 李離者，晉文公之理也。

translations.literal: Li Li was Jin Wen Gong's judge.
translations.idiomatic: Li Li was the chief judge under Duke Wen of Jin.

sentences.zh: 過聽殺人，自拘當死。

translations.literal: Mistakenly heard killed person, self-bound deserved death.
translations.idiomatic: Having mistakenly relied on false testimony and sentenced an innocent man to death, he placed himself under arrest and declared he deserved to die.

sentences.zh: 文公曰：「官有貴賤，罰有輕重。

translations.literal: Wen Gong said: 'Official has noble base, punishment has light heavy.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Wen said, 'There are ranks among officials, and degrees of punishment.

sentences.zh: 下吏有過，非子之罪也。」

translations.literal: Lower official has error, not son's crime.'
translations.idiomatic: A subordinate official made the error; it is not your crime.'

sentences.zh: 李離曰：「臣居官為長，不與吏讓位；

translations.literal: Li Li said: 'Minister dwell official as chief, not with officials yield position;
translations.idiomatic: Li Li said, 'I hold the office of chief and have never yielded my position to a subordinate;

sentences.zh: 受祿為多，不與下分利。

translations.literal: Receive salary as many, not with below divide profit.
translations.idiomatic: I receive the highest salary and have never shared my emoluments with those below me.

sentences.zh: 今過聽殺人，傅其罪下吏，非所聞也。」

translations.literal: Now mistaken heard kill person, shift crime to lower official, not what heard.'
translations.idiomatic: Now that I have mistakenly condemned a man to death, to shift the blame onto a subordinate—that is something I have never heard of.'

sentences.zh: 辭不受令。

translations.literal: Declined not accept order.
translations.idiomatic: He declined and refused the duke's order.

sentences.zh: 文公曰：「子則自以為有罪，寡人亦有罪邪？」

translations.literal: Wen Gong said: 'Son then self consider have crime, sha ren also have crime?'
translations.idiomatic: Duke Wen said, 'If you consider yourself guilty, then am I not guilty as well?'

sentences.zh: 李離曰：「理有法，失刑則刑，失死則死。

translations.literal: Li Li said: 'Judge has law, lose punishment then punish, lose death then death.
translations.idiomatic: Li Li said, 'A judge has his code: if he wrongly sentences a man to punishment, he himself is punished; if he wrongly sentences a man to death, he himself must die.

sentences.zh: 公以臣能聽微決疑，故使為理。

translations.literal: Gong with minister able listen subtle decide doubt, therefore make judge.
translations.idiomatic: Your Grace appointed me as judge because you believed I could discern the subtle and resolve the doubtful.

sentences.zh: 今過聽殺人，罪當死。」

translations.literal: Now mistaken heard kill person, crime deserves death.'
translations.idiomatic: Now I have mistakenly condemned an innocent man to death. My crime warrants death.'

sentences.zh: 遂不受令，伏劍而死。

translations.literal: Then not accept order, prostrate sword die.
translations.idiomatic: He refused the order and fell upon his sword.

translations.literal: Li Li was Jin Wen Gong's judge. Mistakenly heard killed person, self-bound deserved death. Wen Gong said: 'Official has noble base, punishment has light heavy. Lower official has error, not son's crime.' Li Li said: 'Minister dwell official as chief, not with officials yield position; Receive salary as many, not with below divide profit. Now mistaken heard kill person, shift crime to lower official, not what heard.' Declined not accept order. Wen Gong said: 'Son then self consider have crime, sha ren also have crime?' Li Li said: 'Judge has law, lose punishment then punish, lose death then death. Gong with minister able listen subtle decide doubt, therefore make judge. Now mistaken heard kill person, crime deserves death.' Then not accept order, prostrate sword die.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：孫叔敖出一言，郢市復。

translations.literal: Grand Historian said: Sun Shu Ao exit one word, Ying market restore.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: Sun Shuao spoke a single word, and the market of Ying was restored.

sentences.zh: 子產病死，鄭民號哭。

translations.literal: Zi Chan sick die, Zheng people wail cry.
translations.idiomatic: When Zi Chan fell ill and died, the people of Zheng wailed in grief.

sentences.zh: 公儀子見好布而家婦逐。

translations.literal: Gong Yi Zi see good cloth yet family woman expel.
translations.idiomatic: When Gongyi Xiu saw fine cloth in his home, he expelled his wife.

sentences.zh: 石奢縱父而死，楚昭名立。

translations.literal: Shi She release father yet die, Chu Zhao name establish.
translations.idiomatic: Shi She released his father and chose to die, and the reputation of King Zhao of Chu was established.

sentences.zh: 李離過殺而伏劍，晉文以正國法。

translations.literal: Li Li mistaken kill yet prostrate sword, Jin Wen with correct state law.
translations.idiomatic: Li Li wrongly condemned a man and fell upon his sword, and through this Duke Wen of Jin upheld the laws of the state.

translations.literal: Grand Historian said: Sun Shu Ao exit one word, Ying market restore. Zi Chan sick die, Zheng people wail cry. Gong Yi Zi see good cloth yet family woman expel. Shi She release father yet die, Chu Zhao name establish. Li Li mistaken kill yet prostrate sword, Jin Wen with correct state law.
